solution:
```python
def minEnd(n: int, x: int) -> int:
    nums = [1] * n
    for i in range(1, n):
        nums[i] = nums[i] & (nums[i - 1] << 1)
    return nums[-1]
```

efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
We initialize an array `nums` with all elements set to 1. Then, we iterate from the second element to the last, performing bitwise AND operation with the previous element shifted left by 1 bit. This ensures that the resulting array satisfies the given constraints. Finally, we return the last element of the array.
